Dnia | Główne dania
/ Węgier / Gula
GOTOWANIE 45 MIN

Paprikash

A rich Hungarian beef stew with paprika, sour cream, and tender vegetables.

Ingredients:
800g beef chuck, cut into 3cm cubes
3 tablespoons Hungarian paprika
1 large onion, roughly chopped
3 cloves garlic, minced
2 red bell peppers, sliced
200g mushrooms, quartered
1 medium carrot, chunked
400ml beef stock
200ml sour cream
2 tablespoons tomato paste
1 tablespoon vegetable oil
2 bay leaves
Salt and pepper to taste
Fresh parsley for garnish
Egg noodles for serving

Method:
Heat oil in heavy pot over medium-high heat. Brown beef in batches, 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove and set aside. Add chopped onion and garlic, cook until softened, about 3 minutes.

Stir in paprika and tomato paste, cook for 1 minute to toast spices. Return beef to pot,
add stock, bay leaves, salt, and pepper. Bring to simmer, cover, and cook for 30 minutes.

Add bell peppers, mushrooms, and carrot. Continue simmering for 15 minutes until vegetables
are tender and beef is very soft. Remove from heat, stir in sour cream gently.

Adjust seasoning. Serve over cooked egg noodles, garnished with fresh parsley. This stew is
excellent made a day ahead and reheated.

Serves: 4-6
Preparation: 15 minutes
Cooking: 45 minutes
